message:
fix(server/tui): make MacroEngine persistence fallible and collision-safe

The `MacroEngine` previously silently swallowed every persistence
error: `save_macro` used `let _ = std::fs::create_dir_all(...)` and
`let _ = std::fs::write(...)`, `delete_macro` used `let _ =
std::fs::remove_file(...)`, and `rename_macro` did the same. A disk
full, permission error, or serializer failure would all produce a
"success" toast in the TUI while the underlying state silently
diverged between in-memory `self.macros` and the on-disk
`storage_dir`.

On top of that, the pre-existing `sanitize_filename` normalization
can map two distinct macro names onto the same on-disk file
(e.g. `my workflow` and `my/workflow` both sanitize to
`my_workflow.json`), so a second `save_macro` could silently
overwrite an unrelated macro's file.

## New error type

`MacroError` models the five things that can go wrong:

- `NotFound(name)` — rename / delete on a missing macro
- `InvalidName(name)` — empty or whitespace-only names
- `FilenameCollision { name, existing_name }` — two distinct
  in-memory names would land on the same sanitized filename
- `Io(std::io::Error)` — filesystem failure
- `Serialize(serde_json::Error)` — JSON encoding failure

`Display` and `std::error::Error::source` are implemented so the
error can be surfaced in TUI toasts and in downstream log sinks.

## API changes

- `stop_recording(name) -> Result<Option<MacroDef>, MacroError>` —
  now returns `Ok(None)` only for the "no steps recorded" and
  "not recording" cases; any persistence failure is `Err`. The
  recorder state is cleared *before* the `Ok(None)` early return
  but *after* a successful save, so a failed save does not leave
  the engine stuck in `Recording`. The step buffer is also cloned
  instead of `take`n until the save succeeds, so a retry is
  possible without re-recording from scratch.
- `save_macro(def) -> Result<(), MacroError>` — fallible, used
  internally by both `stop_recording` and `rename_macro`.
- `delete_macro(name) -> Result<(), MacroError>` — returns
  `NotFound` for an unknown name, surfaces IO errors, and only
  removes the in-memory entry after the on-disk file is gone.
- `rename_macro(old, new) -> Result<(), MacroError>` — validates
  the new name, rejects filename collisions against other macros
  (excluding the old name itself), writes the renamed macro file
  first, only *then* deletes the old file, and on a remove failure
  cleans up the partially-written new file before returning the
  error. No-op renames (`old == new`) succeed without touching
  disk.

## Filename collision detection

A new private helper `conflicting_name_for_path(name,
exclude_name)` walks the in-memory macro map and returns the first
*other* macro name whose `macro_path(...)` equals the target path.
`stop_recording`, `delete_macro`, and `rename_macro` all call this
before touching disk so a collision produces a deterministic error
instead of a silent overwrite.

## Name validation

`validate_macro_name` rejects empty or whitespace-only names. This
complements the existing `sanitize_filename` normalization, which
would otherwise produce an empty filename.

## `tui_app.rs` call sites

The `macro_ids::RECORD_STOP` and `macro_ids::DELETE_PREFIX`
branches now match on the new `Result`/`Result<Option<...>>`
returns and emit three distinct toasts:

- `Info` — "Saved ..." / "Deleted ..."
- `Warning` — "No steps recorded"
- `Error` — "Failed to save macro: {error}" / "Failed to delete
  macro \"{name}\": {error}"

Two E2E tests in the same file that previously called
`rename_macro(...)` as a `bool` are updated to assert
`.is_ok()`; the semantics are identical because those test fixtures
never hit an error condition.
